calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-10-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Shelton for James Gilyard (Petitioner/Executor).

The Court raised the issue of the estate paying off the loan on the jeep, stated that it understands that it was paid off to save the estate the cost of interest on the loan, questions why the other Beneficiaries should bear the cost of paying off the loan when it is for the benefit of the recipient of the jeep and raised the issue of the accounting being out of balance by $800.00. The Court also noted that the accounting stated that the market value of the jeep was $16,500.00, but the schedule of distribution assigned a value of $13,200.00, that $3,300.00 worth of the jeep value disappeared by the time of distribution and that this again reduces the amount of distribution to the Beneficiaries. Mr. Shelton stated that the Executor is taking two vehicles in lieu of a cash payment from the estate, that the Executor has agreed to put forth his own cash to make his Siblings' shares whole, that everyone will get their fair share, clarified that $16,500.00 is the inventory and appraisal value of the jeep irrespective of the loan and stated that he believes the second amended report suggests that the jeep will be valued at $16,500.00. Mr. Shelton also stated that the accounting problems should be handled as a surcharge, that the Executor is neutral on the issue of deducting $800.00 from his fees or reimbursing the estate, and asked the Court to deduct $800.00 from the Executor's fees. The Second Amended First Account and Final Report and Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
